title: Logical dependency DAG for RiemannHypothesisViaWarpedStationarity.tex (sub-strip Omega_delta version)
description: Machine-checkable encoding of the proof in 'Zeros of zeta on the Critical Line via the Omer-Torresani Unitary Warping of the Hardy Z-Function' culminating in Theorem thm:main (Riemann Hypothesis). Structure: parametric sub-strip Omega_delta = {|Im t| <= 1/2 - delta} with delta-admissible shift c > c_**(delta), strip factorization Z(t) = sqrt(Theta'(t)) Y(Theta(t)) on Omega_delta, reality of zeros of Y via Paley-Wiener + Akhiezer-Levin, transfer to zeros of Z on Omega_delta, and for each nontrivial zero rho = beta + i*gamma the choice delta_rho = min(beta,1-beta)/2 places t_rho = gamma + i(1/2 - beta) inside Omega_{delta_rho}, forcing beta = 1/2.

AX-Digamma.name: Digamma series and positivity of Im psi'(1/4+it/2) for t>0
AX-Digamma.statement: psi'(s) = sum_{k>=0}(s+k)^{-2} for s not in {0,-1,-2,...}; at s = 1/4 + it/2, t>0, Im(s+k)^{-2} = -2(1/4+k)(t/2)/|s+k|^4 < 0 term by term, so Im psi'(1/4+it/2) < 0 and vartheta''(t) = -(1/4) Im psi'(1/4+it/2) > 0

AX-Olver.name: Olver Stirling expansion for psi
AX-Olver.statement: psi(z) admits a Stirling expansion holomorphic in the sector |arg z| < pi - epsilon; applied at z = 1/4 + it/2 gives vartheta'(t) = (1/2) log(|t|/(2pi)) + E(t) with |E(t)| = O(|t|^{-1}) uniformly on any sub-strip Omega_delta away from a fixed bounded set
AX-Olver.source: Olver, Asymptotics and Special Functions, Ch. 8 Sec. 4, Thm. 4.1

AX-Zreal.name: Z is real on R
AX-Zreal.statement: Z(t) = e^{i vartheta(t)} zeta(1/2 + it) is real-valued for t in R; Z is holomorphic on the strip |Im t| < 1/2

AX-zeta-holo.name: Holomorphy of zeta(1/2+it) on the strip
AX-zeta-holo.statement: zeta is holomorphic on C \ {1}; the map t -> zeta(1/2+it) is holomorphic except at t = -i/2, which satisfies |Im(-i/2)| = 1/2
AX-zeta-holo.source: Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. 2

AX-FE-zero.name: Zeros of zeta at rho = beta + i*gamma give zeros of the holomorphic Z at gamma + i(1/2 - beta)
AX-FE-zero.statement: For a nontrivial zero rho = beta + i*gamma of zeta with 0 < beta < 1, the holomorphic extension of Z to the strip |Im t| < 1/2 vanishes at t_rho = gamma + i(1/2 - beta): Z(t_rho) = e^{i vartheta(t_rho)} zeta(1/2 + i t_rho) = e^{i vartheta(t_rho)} zeta(rho) = 0
AX-FE-zero.source: Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. 2; direct substitution using AX-zeta-holo and AX-Zreal

AX-HL.name: Hardy-Littlewood second moment
AX-HL.statement: integral_0^T |zeta(1/2 + it)|^2 dt = T log(T/(2 pi)) + (2 gamma - 1) T + O(T^{1/2} log T)
AX-HL.source: Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. VII Thm. 7.3; Hardy-Littlewood, Acta Math. 41 (1918)

AX-RS.name: Riemann-Siegel formula with phase-derivative remainder bound
AX-RS.statement: Z(t) = 2 sum_{n <= N(t)} n^{-1/2} cos(vartheta(t) - t log n) + R(t) with N(t) = floor(sqrt(t/(2 pi))), R(t) = O(t^{-1/4}), and (d/dt) arg R(t) = O(t^{-1/2})
AX-RS.source: Edwards, Riemann's Zeta Function, Sec. 7.4 Thm. 7.1; Gabcke Dissertation Goettingen 1979 Satz 7.1; Berry, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 450 (1995) eq. 18

AX-Bochner.name: Bochner theorem for positive-definite continuous functions
AX-Bochner.statement: A continuous positive-definite function on R is the Fourier transform of a finite non-negative Borel measure on R

AX-WK.name: Wiener Theorem 29 (spectral measure via windowed Fourier transform)
AX-WK.statement: For time-average WSS f with covariance c_f the Fourier transform of mu_f, and K_T(mu) := (2 pi)^{-1} integral_0^T f(u) e^{-i mu u} du, for bounded Borel E in R: mu_f(E) = lim_{T->inf} (2 pi / T) integral_E |K_T(mu)|^2 d mu

AX-PW-WSS.name: Paley-Wiener for time-average WSS functions with compactly supported spectral measure
AX-PW-WSS.statement: If f : R -> C is time-average WSS with covariance c_f(eta) = integral e^{i eta xi} d mu_f(xi) and supp mu_f is contained in [-sigma, sigma], then f extends to an entire function of exponential type at most sigma with |f(z)| <= mu_f(R)^{1/2} e^{sigma |Im z|} on C
AX-PW-WSS.source: Yaglom, Correlation Theory of Stationary and Related Random Functions, Vol. I, Thm. 4.7.3; Bochner-Phillips, Ann. Math. 43 (1942), 409-418

AX-Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer-Levin Laguerre-Polya criterion
AX-Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function F of exponential type at most tau whose autocorrelation measure (Fourier transform of |F|^2 as tempered distribution) is non-negative, finite, and supported in [-2 tau, 2 tau] belongs to the Laguerre-Polya class; every complex zero of F is real
AX-Akhiezer.source: Akhiezer, Theory of Approximation, Sec. V.4; Levin, Lectures on Entire Functions, Lecture 16 Thm. 3

AX-IdentityThm.name: Identity theorem for holomorphic functions
AX-IdentityThm.statement: Two holomorphic functions on a connected open set that agree on a set with an accumulation point (in particular on R intersected with the set) coincide on the set
AX-IdentityThm.source: Ahlfors, Complex Analysis, 3rd ed., Ch. 4 Sec. 3.2

lem:analstrip.name: Analyticity of vartheta' on sub-strips
lem:analstrip.statement: For every delta in (0, 1/2), vartheta' extends holomorphically to an open neighborhood of Omega_delta; the poles of psi(1/4 + it/2) at t = i(2k + 1/2) all satisfy |Im t| >= 1/2 > 1/2 - delta

lem:olver.name: Olver expansion on sub-strips
lem:olver.statement: For every delta in (0, 1/2) there exist A_0(delta) > 0 and M(delta) > 0 such that on {t in Omega_delta : |Re t| >= A_0(delta)}, vartheta'(t) = (1/2) log(|t|/(2 pi)) + E(t) with |E(t)| <= M(delta)/|t|; in particular Re vartheta'(t) -> +infinity uniformly on Omega_delta as |Re t| -> infinity

def:admissibledelta.name: Admissible shift for delta
def:admissibledelta.statement: For delta in (0, 1/2), choose A(delta) > A_0(delta) with (1/2) log(A(delta)/(2 pi)) - M(delta)/A(delta) > 2; define c_**(delta) := sup over {t in Omega_delta : |Re t| <= A(delta)} of |vartheta'(t)| + 1, finite by Lemma lem:analstrip and Lemma lem:olver; a shift c is delta-admissible if c > c_**(delta)

lem:Thetabij.name: Monotone phase shift on Omega_delta
lem:Thetabij.statement: Fix delta in (0, 1/2) and a delta-admissible c. The shifted phase Theta(t) = vartheta(t) + c t satisfies (i) Theta'(t) > 0 for all t in R with Theta : R -> R a C^infty bijection, (ii) Theta' is holomorphic on Omega_delta with Re Theta'(t) >= 1, (iii) Theta : Omega_delta -> Theta(Omega_delta) is a holomorphic bijection, (iv) there is a single-valued holomorphic branch of sqrt(Theta') on Omega_delta positive on R, (v) Theta^{-1}(R) intersect Omega_delta = R

def:DOT.name: Omer-Torresani warping operator
def:DOT.statement: For a C^1 diffeomorphism gamma : R -> R with gamma' > 0, [D_gamma x](t) := sqrt(gamma'(t)) x(gamma(t))

lem:unitary.name: Unitarity and composition of D_gamma
lem:unitary.statement: D_gamma : L^2(R) -> L^2(R) is unitary with D_gamma^{-1} = D_{gamma^{-1}}; for diffeomorphisms gamma, phi: D_phi o D_gamma = D_{gamma o phi}

lem:Yreal.name: Y real on R
lem:Yreal.statement: Y(u) in R for every u in R

def:covg.name: Time-average covariance
def:covg.statement: c_f(eta) := lim_{U->inf} U^{-1} integral_0^U f(u) conj(f(u+eta)) du when the limit exists

lem:HL.name: Hardy-Littlewood second moment (paper statement)
lem:HL.statement: integral_0^T |zeta(1/2 + it)|^2 dt = T log(T/(2 pi)) + (2 gamma - 1) T + O(T^{1/2} log T)

lem:RS.name: Riemann-Siegel expansion (paper statement)
lem:RS.statement: Z(t) = 2 sum_{n <= N(t)} n^{-1/2} cos(vartheta(t) - t log n) + R(t), N(t) = floor(sqrt(t/(2 pi))), R(t) = O(t^{-1/4}), (d/dt) arg R(t) = O(t^{-1/2})

thm:Ywss.name: Time-average WSS of Y
thm:Ywss.statement: c_Y(eta) exists for every eta in R and depends only on eta; c_Y(0) = 2

lem:omegabound.name: Instantaneous u-frequency ratio
lem:omegabound.statement: For every n >= 1 and t >= 2 pi n^2, omega_n(t) := (vartheta'(t) - log n)/(vartheta'(t) + c) lies in [0, 1). Non-negativity: vartheta'' > 0 on (0, infinity) (AX-Digamma) and vartheta'(2 pi n^2) = log n + O(n^{-2}) (Olver) give vartheta'(t) >= log n for t >= 2 pi n^2. Strict upper bound: (vartheta'(t) + c) - (vartheta'(t) - log n) = c + log n > 0, so omega_n(t) = 1 - (c + log n)/(vartheta'(t) + c) < 1

cor:Cramer.name: Bochner spectral measure of Y
cor:Cramer.statement: There is a finite non-negative Borel measure mu_Y on R with c_Y(eta) = integral e^{i eta xi} d mu_Y(xi) and mu_Y(R) = 2

def:KT.name: Windowed Fourier transform and power spectral density
def:KT.statement: K_T(mu) := (2 pi)^{-1} integral_0^T Z(t) sqrt(Theta'(t)) e^{-i mu Theta(t)} dt; S(mu) := limsup_{T->inf} 2 pi |K_T(mu)|^2 / Theta(T)

thm:bandlim.name: Band-limit theorem
thm:bandlim.statement: For every delta in (0, 1/2), every delta-admissible c, and every mu with |mu| > 1: S(mu) = 0. Proof uses the instantaneous u-frequency bound |omega_n(t)| < 1 (Lemma lem:omegabound), the exact cancellation of vartheta''(t(u)) between numerator and denominator of the IBP amplitude-over-phase-derivative ratio Q = x^{1/2}/((sigma - mu) x - sigma(c + log n)) under the substitution x = Theta'(t(u)), a single integration by parts per mode with boundary O(beta_n^{-1/2}) and tail O(beta_n^{-1/2}), and the remainder IBP using (d/dt) arg R = O(t^{-1/2})

thm:PW.name: Paley-Wiener entire extension of Y
thm:PW.statement: Y extends to an entire function of exponential type at most 1 with |Y(z)| <= sqrt(2) e^{|Im z|} on C

thm:Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer-Laguerre-Polya criterion (paper statement)
thm:Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function F of exponential type at most tau whose time-average autocorrelation measure is finite, non-negative, and supported in [-2 tau, 2 tau] lies in the Laguerre-Polya class and has all complex zeros real

thm:LP.name: Reality of zeros of Y
thm:LP.statement: All complex zeros of the entire extension of Y are real

thm:stripfact.name: Strip factorization on Omega_delta
thm:stripfact.statement: For every delta in (0, 1/2) and every delta-admissible c, Z(t) = sqrt(Theta'(t)) Y(Theta(t)) for every t in Omega_delta, with Y the entire extension of thm:PW, Theta' the holomorphic extension on Omega_delta, and sqrt(Theta') the branch of Lemma lem:Thetabij (iv). Proof: both sides are holomorphic on the connected open Omega_delta (left side uses AX-zeta-holo and lem:analstrip; right side uses lem:Thetabij (iii)(iv) and thm:PW); agreement on R from Definition def:Y; identity theorem AX-IdentityThm extends equality to Omega_delta

thm:zero-bij.name: Zero-set bijection on Omega_delta
thm:zero-bij.statement: t_0 in Omega_delta is a zero of Z of order m iff Theta(t_0) is a zero of Y of order m

cor:Zreal.name: Reality of zeros of Z on Omega_delta
cor:Zreal.statement: Every zero of Z in Omega_delta lies on R

thm:main.name: Riemann Hypothesis
thm:main.statement: Every nontrivial zero rho of zeta satisfies Re rho = 1/2. Proof: for rho = beta + i*gamma with 0 < beta < 1, set delta_rho = (1/2) min(beta, 1 - beta) and t_rho = gamma + i(1/2 - beta); then |Im t_rho| = 1/2 - 2 delta_rho < 1/2 - delta_rho so t_rho is in Omega_{delta_rho}; choose a delta_rho-admissible c (def:admissibledelta); strip factorization (thm:stripfact) and AX-FE-zero give Z(t_rho) = 0; cor:Zreal forces t_rho in R, hence Im t_rho = 1/2 - beta = 0, so beta = 1/2

notes: Sub-strip Omega_delta = {|Im t| <= 1/2 - delta}; shift c > c_**(delta) gives Re Theta'(t) >= 1 on Omega_delta.

Primary chain: lem:analstrip, lem:olver -> def:admissibledelta -> lem:Thetabij; def:DOT -> lem:unitary; lem:Thetabij + lem:unitary -> def:Y -> lem:Yreal; def:covg + lem:HL + lem:RS + lem:omegabound -> thm:Ywss -> cor:Cramer; def:KT + lem:RS + lem:omegabound -> thm:bandlim -> cor:supp; thm:Ywss + cor:Cramer + cor:supp -> thm:PW; thm:PW + lem:Yreal + thm:Akhiezer + cor:supp -> thm:LP; def:Y + lem:Thetabij + lem:analstrip + thm:PW + AX-zeta-holo + AX-IdentityThm -> thm:stripfact -> thm:zero-bij -> cor:Zreal; def:admissibledelta + thm:stripfact + cor:Zreal + AX-FE-zero -> thm:main (contradiction using t_rho in Omega_{delta_rho}).

The two additions over the prior height-adapted version: (1) lem:omegabound makes the instantaneous u-frequency ratio identification omega_n(t) in [0,1) explicit, used by both thm:Ywss and thm:bandlim; (2) thm:bandlim now records the exact cancellation of vartheta''(t(u)) in the IBP ratio Q = x^{1/2}/((sigma - mu) x - sigma(c + log n)) via the substitution x = Theta'(t(u)).
Axioms: AX-Digamma, AX-Olver, AX-Zreal, AX-zeta-holo, AX-FE-zero, AX-HL, AX-RS, AX-Bochner, AX-WK, AX-PW-WSS, AX-Akhiezer, AX-IdentityThm.
